diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling Genshin Impact Player Support, this is Alex. How can I help?
[Caller] The character banner ends in two days and I'm at pity 68 with no 5-star. If I don't pull him before it ends, do I lose my pity?
[Agent] Great question, and a really common source of stress right before banner transitions. Short answer: you do not lose your pity. Let me pull your account so I can be specific. UID?
[Caller] 703321876.
[Agent] Thanks, Liam. I see you're at 68 pulls on the current character event banner, and you've got your 50/50 still available. Here's how carryover works: pity counters on the character event banner pool as one continuous counter. When this banner ends and the next character banner begins, your 68 pulls travel with you. Your 50/50 status travels too. You won't lose a single pull.
[Caller] Even if the next banner is a different character?
[Agent] Exactly right. So if you don't pull him before the banner ends, your 68 pulls start you almost inside soft-pity territory on whoever's featured next. Same applies to the weapon banner with its own separate counter, and the standard banner with its own. They don't cross-pollinate, but within each pool, progress always carries.
[Caller] OK that's a huge relief. One more — does the 50/50 I'm holding also carry?
[Agent] Yes. If you're on a 'guaranteed next' from a previous 50/50 loss, that guarantee carries to future character event banners too. It never expires until you cash it in.
[Caller] Perfect. So realistically, should I pull now or wait?
[Agent] That's a personal call, but here's the framing: if you specifically want this character, pull now and you're in soft-pity range — high chance of landing. If you're lukewarm on him and saving for someone upcoming, hold — your pity is safe. Never pull out of fear of losing progress, because you won't lose progress.
[Caller] Got it. I actually really want him, so I'll commit tonight.
[Agent] Sounds good. I'll email you a quick reference on pity carryover rules for your records. Anything else?
[Caller] No, that's perfect. Thanks Alex.
[Agent] Good luck, Liam — I hope he shows up early!
